daily_brief: The US-Israel war on Iran entered its fifth week with no signs of de-escalation, as Iranian strikes hit major industrial facilities across the Gulf region, including aluminium plants in the UAE and Bahrain, and damaged Kuwait's international airport radar system [BBC, Financial Times, The Hindu]. A UNIFIL peacekeeper from Indonesia was killed by a projectile in southern Lebanon, the latest casualty as the conflict spreads [Le Monde, France 24]. Pakistan announced it will host US-Iran talks in coming days, though Tehran's parliament speaker dismissed the diplomatic effort as cover for potential ground operations [Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. In Paris, three people were arrested following an attempted bomb attack on Bank of America's headquarters, with French authorities suggesting possible links to Iranian proxies [BBC, France 24]. President Trump softened his rhetoric on Cuba, allowing a Russian oil tanker carrying 730,000 barrels of crude to deliver relief to the crisis-hit island despite Washington's de facto blockade [Le Monde, France 24]. Meanwhile, Israel reversed its decision to block the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem from the Church of the Holy Sepulchre after international outcry over the Palm Sunday incident [BBC, France 24]. The G7 is holding crisis talks as the Iran war continues to roil the global economy [Financial Times].

top_stories.headline: Iranian Strikes Hit Industrial Sites Across Gulf as War Enters Fifth Week
bullets: Iran's IRGC launched drone and missile attacks on aluminium facilities in UAE and Bahrain, wounding workers at both sites
Kuwait's international airport radar system was significantly damaged by 15 drones; Oman is investigating a drone strike on its southern port of Salalah
Iran claimed the strikes targeted facilities with American military links, in response to US-Israeli attacks on Iranian steel production sites
Gulf nations are insisting they must have a say in any future settlement as the war threatens their economies and way of life

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on Gulf nations' concerns and the economic implications for the region

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Emphasized G7 crisis talks and global economic impact

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Reported Australian PM's call for clarity on US war objectives

top_stories.headline: UNIFIL Peacekeeper Killed in Southern Lebanon as Israel-Hezbollah Fighting Intensifies
bullets: An Indonesian peacekeeper was killed and three others wounded when a projectile hit a UNIFIL position near Adchit al Qusayr in southern Lebanon
The origin of the projectile is under investigation; UN chief Guterres condemned the killing as part of a pattern of incidents jeopardizing peacekeeper safety
Three Lebanese journalists were also killed Saturday in a targeted Israeli strike in Jezzine; Israel claimed one was a Hezbollah operative, providing no evidence
France's foreign minister said deliberate targeting of journalists would be a 'blatant violation of international law'

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on casualty details and investigation

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Included Israeli military justification for journalist strike alongside denials

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Highlighted French government condemnation and Iran's IRGC response dedicating strikes to slain journalists

top_stories.headline: Pakistan to Host US-Iran Talks as Tehran Warns Against Ground Attack
bullets: Pakistan announced it will host talks between the US and Iran in coming days, following weeks of quiet diplomacy
Foreign ministers from Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia met in Islamabad to discuss the crisis
Iran's parliament speaker dismissed the talks as cover, warning Iranian forces are ready to 'set American troops on fire' if ground operations begin
Iran also threatened to attack homes of US and Israeli commanders and political officials in the region

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Detailed diplomatic context and Pakistan's mediating role

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iranian warnings and Australian concerns about war objectives

top_stories.headline: Three Arrested in Paris After Attempted Bomb Attack on Bank of America
bullets: A suspect was detained after placing a device containing five litres of fuel and an ignition system near Bank of America's Paris headquarters early Saturday
Two additional suspects were arrested Sunday; the incident occurred near the Champs-Élysées
French Interior Minister suggested the attack could be linked to Iranian services acting through proxies amid the US-Israel war on Iran
The Iranian embassy in France has not commented on the allegations

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced reporting with official statements from both French authorities

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Similar coverage with emphasis on ongoing investigation

top_stories.headline: Israel Reverses Ban on Latin Patriarch at Holy Sepulchre After International Outcry
bullets: Israeli police prevented Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre for Palm Sunday Mass, citing security concerns during the Iran war
The incident marked the first time in centuries a Latin Patriarch was barred from the site on Palm Sunday
US Ambassador Mike Huckabee called it an 'unfortunate overreach' that was 'difficult to understand or justify'
Prime Minister Netanyahu announced 'full and immediate access' would be restored following widespread international condemnation

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage including US diplomatic criticism

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on the restoration of access and international reaction
